rationale: The cited paper studies two constructs central to the citing paper—brand conspicuousness and negative spillover to the genuine brand (preference erosion after counterfeit exposure)—which closely parallels the citing paper’s focus on conspicuous usage causing brand dilution. The citing note also frames its contribution as extending prior dilution/conspicuousness research, making this citation plausibly foundational or a direct comparison point rather than an obvious throwaway mention. With this conceptual alignment, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
